meta.task: Task A — Hecke Search: Non-CM Newforms k=6, N∈[50,500]

cm_forms_k6.reason: CM k=6 forms have |a_137| ~ 2*137^(5/2) ≈ 439371 (split primes) or |a_137|=0 (inert primes). Both cases miss the target window [1000, 81400].

noncm_k6_level_le4.reason: Only 3 eta-product newforms exist at N≤4 for k=6; all are CM eta products. No non-CM forms at these levels.

sato_tate_analysis.interpretation: Statistically ~1 match expected in N∈[50,500]; neither confirmed nor ruled out without direct computation.

network_status.reason: Sandboxed environment without external network access or SageMath installation.

noncm_k6_level_50_to_500.reason: Requires LMFDB API access or SageMath CuspForms(N,6).newforms('a') for each N.

noncm_k6_level_5_to_49.reason: Dimension very small (<15 forms total); low probability of match.

verdict_note: The search for non-CM k=6 newforms at N∈[50,500] is structurally possible (Sato-Tate does not forbid it) but computationally incomplete. No match was found or ruled out within the available (offline) computation. The search gap is precisely characterised: all non-CM k=6 newforms at N∈[50,500] remain unsearched. CM forms at all levels are ruled out by structural argument. A definitive verdict requires LMFDB API access or SageMath. Expected ~0.84 matches; neither confirmed nor excluded.

next_actions.action: Run search_hecke_lmfdb_api.py with LMFDB network access
If no match at N≤500, extend to N≤2000 and document as DEAD_END
